Reported 4Q PAT of Rs7.6bn vs an Rs8-9bn run-rate over past few quarters
This was driven by mark-to-market (MTM) losses on its investment portfolio; excluding this, operating profit was 2% higher QoQ.
It was a steady quarter for asset management industry, with flows, both SIP and lump-sum, remaining strong.
IPRU AMC’s mutual fund quarterly-average AUM (QAAUM) grew 3% QoQ/26% YoY to Rs11tn.
More importantly, active equity AUM grew 2% QoQ and its market share improved 40bp.
This is healthy given the macro backdrop since Middle East conflict began in March.
Management commented that it is not witnessing any slowdown in equity sales in April, either.

*CITI on ICICI Pru MF*
Buy, TP Rs 3900
4Q core PBT was in-line at Rs11.3bn (+2% QoQ).
Sluggish trends in AAUM accretion sequentially, largely led by tepid equity market sentiment, & unfavourable mix shift (ex-hybrid equity and equity-oriented MF AAUM mix down 70bps QoQ) driving marginal moderation in MF yields, led to a sharp slowdown in core PBT growth QoQ.
Lower employee expense QoQ nevertheless aided core profitability.
Flow trends held up well, capturing robust performance trends of most schemes (flow market share > AAUM market share in equities).
Management articulated concerted efforts towards expanding its product basket in SIF and alternates, which is likely to further fuel growth.
Management highlighted efforts to reduce the net impact (3-4bps on gross basis) of reduction in net expense ratio, in MF, from Apr’26 – foresee a negligible impact, if any.

GPIL signed huge 5-year deal with big China company EVE Power, to make 20 GWh of storage systems of about $1 billion :)

Your factory needs electricity to run machines. Every month you get an electricity bill from the government power company (called DISCOM).

Let's say your bill comes to ₹10 per unit. That's expensive. It eats your profit.

What these days Corporate doing, if yu read their filings is:

There is Rule 3 of the Electricity Rules, 2005 (under the Electricity Act, 2003). To be called captive generating plant and get the benefits, two conditions must be met:

The consumer (or group of consumers) must hold at least 26% of the equity of the power-generating company.

That same consumer must consume at least 51% of the power generated annually. 

If you have skin in the game (26%) and you actually use the power (51%), then we'll treat you like you built your own power plant — even though you didn't. And we'll waive all the taxes we normally charge for using the grid."

When you buy power from the regular grid (DISCOM), your bill has these layers:

> Base energy charge
> Cross-Subsidy Surcharge (CSS)
> Additional Surcharge (AS) 

CSS + AS alone can be ₹1.5–2.5 per unit on top of base cost. That's why industrial tariffs are so painful.

The captive structure waives CSS and AS completely. This is the legal bypass.

The rule is - If you want to buy cheap solar power directly (not from DISCOM), then you must own at least 26% of the solar plant. And you must use at least 51% of the power it makes 

Small investment → You only pay for 26%, developer pays 74%. Very capital-light.

Government blessing → If you own 26%, government waives extra taxes (called CSS and Additional Surcharge), making it even cheaper.

That is why everyone is doing it:

UltraTech Cement → 26% in a solar SPV
Jindal Stainless → 33% in a solar SPV
JK Lakshmi Cement → 26% in a solar SPV
Motherson → 28% in a solar SPV
GACL → 26% in a solar SPV
Concord Biotech → 26% in a solar SPV
Sanathan Textiles → 26% in a solar SPV

Now Yu have to Connect this with Demand of Green Energy, BESS, why GPIL did partnership with China & How factories cutting cost. labour code will be discounted here 

One have to Understand there will super demand for the next 5 year for energy and Battery is Bottleneck :)

GPIL know what game they are playing, from experiment to Scale up :)

One business is cash cow will fund the other :)

The US housing market is weakening heading into the spring selling season:

Existing home sales fell -3.6% MoM in March, to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 3.98 million, the lowest since June 2025.

This marks the 2nd-largest decline since November 2022.

Sales in the Northeast fell to the lowest on record in data going back to 1999, while the Midwest matched its worst reading since 2011.

The decline occurred before the recent jump in mortgage rates caused by the Iran war, suggesting further weakness ahead.

As a result, the National Association of Realtors lowered its 2026 existing home sales forecast to +4%, from +14% previously.

Affordability remains the primary concern for US homebuyers.

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 14 April 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

GHV INFRA: Wins ₹1,250 Cr expressway EPC order from APCO Infratech in Maharashtra.

MONARCH SURVEYORS: Bags ₹130 Cr railway contract from Northern Railway for survey, DPR & design work.

DCX SYSTEMS: Secures combined orders worth ~₹31.6 Cr for cable harness & PCB assemblies.

TEXMACO: Bags ₹27.18 Cr wagon order from UltraTech Cement.

GANESH GREEN BHARAT: Wins ₹7.34 Cr solar module supply order from Upvoltage Solutions.

RAILTEL: ₹17.12 Cr work order from Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti cancelled due to administrative reasons.

META INFOTECH: Bags ₹1.03 Cr IT support services renewal order from a private bank.

📍 ACQUISITION

L&T: Completes 100% acquisition of IGS Ltd via L&T Realty, strengthening real estate portfolio.

SHARP INDIA: Promoter Sharp Corp to sell 75% stake to Smart Services for ₹19.45 Cr, triggering open offer.

📍 COLLABORATION

PRESTIGE GROUP: Partners with Autodesk for 3-year digital transformation across projects.

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

KILBURN ENGINEERING: Appoints Amritanshu Khaitan as Non-Executive Chairman effective April 21, 2026.

JK TYRE: Appoints Mandar Deo as President – India, strengthening leadership in EV & manufacturing.

FINO PAYMENTS BANK: MD & CEO Rishi Gupta unavailable for 45+ days; interim CEO appointed with RBI approval.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

NAMO E-WASTE: To set up hydrometallurgy plant in Haryana for Li-ion battery recycling by Dec 2026.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

ANAND RATHI: FY26 saw 10.2% revenue growth & 24.8% PAT growth driven by strong non-broking segments.

SHANTI GOLD: Reports strong Q4 with 120% YoY revenue growth and 25% volume growth.

VALENCIA NUTRITION: Maintains strong operations amid Hormuz crisis with robust inventory & cost advantage.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

INFINIUM PHARMACHEM: Gujarat plant temporarily shut by GPCB over environmental compliance issues.

📍 LAUNCHES

AMAGI: Launches AI-driven AdFlow Orchestrator platform to boost FAST TV monetization.

EIMCO ELECON: Launches battery-operated mining vehicles, expanding into electric mining solutions.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

SATIN CREDITCARE: Arm receives SEBI Category II AIF license, entering alternative investment space.

BAJAJ CONSUMER CARE: Receives NCLT approval for demerger with Vishal Personal Care.

📍 ADDITIONS

PRITHVI EXCHANGE: Opens new forex branch in Trivandrum, expanding South India presence.

WEWORK INDIA: Expands Pune capacity by ~864 desks with ₹36 Cr investment.

📍 OTHERS

TATA STEEL: EU to cut tariff-free steel imports and impose up to 50% tariffs, impacting global trade dynamics.

NHPC: Approves monetization of cash flows from Uri-II & Dhauli Ganga assets to unlock liquidity.

JSW INFRA: Commissions Gati Shakti cargo terminal at Arakkonam, boosting logistics capacity.

PICCADILLY: Indri wins ‘Best Special Edition Design’ at World Whiskies Awards 2026.

GLOBAL MARKETS: Investors turn bearish on oil with expectations of lower prices amid growth concerns.

Macquarie On India Fuel Retail

BROKERAGE RADAR

Significant under-recoveries; brace for higher pump prices

At spot price, India's oil marketing companies are effectively subsidising consumers by Rs18/Rs35 per litre on petrol/diesel

While the contribution of excise to government revenue has been declining, it still forms a meaningful ~8% of revenue and ~17% of the fiscal deficit

Top down, see risk of higher pump prices post state elections in April, which will likely pressure margins

text:
ANAND RATHI SHARE: SHIFT FROM BROKING TO DIVERSIFIED REVENUE MODEL

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Strong earnings growth driven by non-broking segments and margin expansion despite muted client growth  

STRUCTURAL SHIFT  
- Transition from broking-led to diversified revenue streams  
- Rising contribution from MTF, distribution, and other income  
- Increasing focus on advisory and long-term investing  
- Expansion into wider geographic footprint (307 cities)  
- Impact: Positive — improving revenue quality  

WHY THIS MATTERS  
- Reduced dependence on cyclical broking income  
- Higher margin segments driving profitability  
- MTF growth indicates rising leverage-led activity  
- Distribution business adds stable fee income  
- Impact: Positive — stronger and more resilient model  

BUSINESS PERFORMANCE

GROWTH TRENDS (Q4FY26)  
- Revenue growth strong at 28.1% YoY  
- EBITDA growth sharp at 51.4% YoY  
- PAT surged 125.7% YoY  
- Impact: Strong earnings momentum  

PROFITABILITY  
- EBITDA margin expanded to 43.2% (vs 36.5%)  
- Operating leverage visible  
- Impact: Positive — margin expansion story  

SEGMENTAL PERFORMANCE

BROKING (WEAKNESS)  
- Growth moderate in Q4 (+14.5%)  
- Decline in FY26 (-6.8%)  
- Impact: Negative — core segment under pressure  

MTF (STRONG DRIVER)  
- Interest income growth strong (+50.2% Q4, +32.6% FY)  
- MTF book up 60.7% YoY  
- Impact: Positive — high growth, high yield segment  

DISTRIBUTION BUSINESS  
- Strong growth (+34–44% YoY)  
- Increasing contribution to total revenue  
- Impact: Positive — stable fee income  

OTHER OPERATING INCOME  
- Strong growth (~43% YoY Q4)  
- Diversification benefits visible  
- Impact: Positive  

ASSET & CLIENT METRICS

AUM & SCALE  
- AUM growth at 20.6% YoY  
- AUC growth at 16.0% YoY  
- Impact: Positive — expanding asset base  

CLIENT METRICS (CONCERN)  
- Active clients declined by 3.9% YoY  
- Indicates weaker retail participation or consolidation  
- Impact: Slight Negative  

MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY INSIGHT  
- Challenging macro environment (FII outflows, global uncertainty)  
- Focus on strengthening client relationships  
- Strategic shift towards advisory-led model  
- Impact: Neutral to Positive — long-term positioning  

MOAT CHARACTERISTICS  
- Diversified revenue streams beyond broking  
- Strong presence across India (307 cities)  
- Growing MTF and distribution franchise  
- Advisory-led client engagement model  
- Impact: Improving competitive positioning  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Company evolving from transaction-driven broking to a more stable, annuity-like revenue model  

OUTLOOK  
- Continued momentum in MTF and distribution segments  
- Broking recovery dependent on market activity  
- Margin sustainability likely due to operating leverage  

IMPACT  
- Positive — strong profitability and diversification offset weakness in core broking business

PAT surged 126% YoY to ₹416 Mn (16% margin).

ICICI PRUDENTIAL LIFE: MARGIN LED QUALITY SHIFT STORY

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Strong profitability driven by margin expansion despite muted new business growth  

STRUCTURAL SHIFT  
- Transition from volume driven growth to profitability focused strategy  
- Shift from savings products to protection mix  
- Increasing focus on capital efficient distribution  
- Digital led operating model strengthening scalability  
- Impact: Positive — improving business quality  

WHY THIS MATTERS  
- Margin expansion offsets weak APE growth  
- Selective underwriting improves long term profitability  
- Protection mix drives higher VNB margins  
- Strong balance sheet ensures stability  
- Impact: Positive — quality over quantity approach  

BUSINESS PERFORMANCE

GROWTH TRENDS  
- APE growth muted at 2.2% YoY  
- Total premium growth healthy at 8.5% YoY  
- Retail weighted premium slightly declined  
- Impact: Moderate growth visibility  

PROFITABILITY  
- PAT growth strong at 34.6% YoY  
- VNB growth at 10.9% YoY  
- VNB margin expanded by 190 bps to 24.7%  
- Impact: Strong margin led earnings  

PRODUCT MIX STRATEGY

HIGH MARGIN SEGMENTS  
- Protection business strong growth (~30% YoY)  
- Retail protection sharply up (~60% YoY)  
- Impact: Positive — premiumisation  

WEAK SEGMENTS  
- Non linked savings declining  
- Annuity segment volatile  
- Impact: Negative — traditional business slowdown  

DISTRIBUTION STRATEGY

SCALABLE CHANNELS  
- Partnership channel strong growth (~23% YoY)  
- Group business growth (~14.5% YoY)  
- Reduced dependence on agency  
- Impact: Positive — capital efficient scaling  

CUSTOMER METRICS

PERSISTENCY  
- 13 month persistency declined to 84.5%  
- Indicates retention or mix related challenges  
- Impact: Slight Negative  

TRUST METRICS  
- Claim settlement ratio at 99.3%  
- Fast settlement turnaround (~1.1 days)  
- Impact: Strong brand credibility  

BALANCE SHEET STRENGTH

FINANCIAL POSITION  
- AUM at ₹3.14 trillion  
- Embedded value growth ~10.5%  
- Solvency ratio strong at 227%  
- Zero NPA track record  
- Impact: Strong — industry leading stability  

COST EFFICIENCY  
- Cost ratios stable and improving

GPIL – Energy Strategy, Captive Power & BESS Opportunity

Big Strategic Move
• GPIL signs $1 Bn, 5-year deal with EVE Power for 20 GWh BESS
• Clear shift from pilot → scale execution in energy storage

Why Companies Are Moving to Captive Power
• DISCOM tariffs high; ₹10/unit+ impacts margins
• Extra charges: CSS + AS (~₹1.5–2.5/unit) inflate costs
• Captive structure removes these charges → direct cost savings

Captive Power Rule Advantage
• Minimum 26% equity stake in power SPV
• Must consume 51% of generated power
• Benefit: Waiver of CSS & AS → cheaper power vs grid

Capital-Efficient Model
• Company invests only 26%
• Developer funds remaining 74%
• Outcome: Low capex, high savings

Industry Adoption Trend
• Widespread adoption across sectors
• Examples:
– UltraTech Cement: 26% stake
– Jindal Stainless: 33% stake
– JK Lakshmi Cement: 26% stake
– Motherson: 28% stake
– GACL / Concord / Sanathan: ~26% stake

Why BESS Becomes Critical
• Renewable energy is intermittent
• Storage ensures 24x7 reliable power
• Battery = key bottleneck in energy transition

GPIL’s Strategic Positioning
• Integrating renewable + storage (BESS)
• Targets industrial customers shifting to captive model
• Positioned in high-growth energy infra segment

Demand Tailwinds (Next 5 Years)
• Massive push for green energy adoption
• Rising industrial power demand
• Grid constraints increasing need for storage solutions

Business Model Insight
• Core business = cash flow generator
• New energy vertical = high growth engine
• Internal funding supports expansion without heavy leverage

Outlook
• Captive + BESS = structural cost advantage for industry
• GPIL entering early in multi-year opportunity
• Strong potential for scalable, high-margin growth

India Data Centre Market – AI & Cloud Driven Expansion

Market Growth
• Market to grow from ~$10 Bn (2025) to $22 Bn by 2030
• Strong demand from AI, cloud, digital consumption

Capacity Expansion
• Current capacity ~1.4–1.6 GW (164 facilities)
• Expected ~1.7–2 GW by 2026
• Long-term expansion to ~4–5 GW by 2030

Investment Pipeline
• $13–15 Bn invested (2020–24); ~80% foreign capital
• Additional $60–70 Bn planned over next 5 years

Key Growth Drivers
• 5G rollout, OTT growth, digital payments
• Data usage rising to ~25GB/user/month

Geographical Mix
• Mumbai leads with ~49% share
• Chennai & NCR key hubs
• Tier-II cities emerging

Cost Advantage
• Build cost $6–7 Mn per MW; globally competitive

Outlook
• Strong infra build-out ahead
• India positioned as a global data centre hub

AI Industry – Compute & Power Crunch

• AI demand surge (agentic AI) straining compute capacity
• Bottleneck shifting to “tokens” → severe shortage

• GPU costs rising; Nvidia Blackwell up ~48% in 2 months
• Infra demand outpacing supply → capacity crunch

• Issues:
– Outages & reliability concerns
– Compute rationing
– Delayed launches / feature cuts

• OpenAI redirected compute; Sora rollout impacted
• Anthropic limiting usage; prioritising enterprise

• Token usage surged (~6 bn → 15 bn/min)

• Cloud contracts tightening; higher costs, longer lock-ins

• Rising costs impacting startups & enterprises

• Reliability pressure; uptime challenges emerging

• Core constraint: Power + chips + infra limits

• Outlook: AI growth capped by compute availability, not demand

Battery Sector – Localisation Push (ALBM)

• Govt to introduce Approved List of Battery Manufacturers (ALBM)
• Only approved vendors eligible for EV & storage projects
• Aim: Reduce imports, strengthen energy security

• Part of India Battery Vision 2047
• Covers mining → cell manufacturing → recycling

• Acts as non-tariff barrier for trusted suppliers
• Target: ~47 GW storage (~$38 bn opportunity)

• Focus on ACC cells + key components
• 5 GWh PLI bids expected soon

• Key areas:
– Critical minerals sourcing
– Cell & pack manufacturing
– Energy management & safety

• Batteries key for grid stability & EV growth

• Challenges: PLI delays, import dependence, R&D gaps

• Outlook: Strong policy push for self-reliant ecosystem

Zepto – Path to Profitability

• Cash burn reduced to ₹850–900 Cr (vs ₹1,200–1,300 Cr)
• Driven by lower per-order cost + slower expansion (~1,100 stores)
• EBITDA loss narrowed to ~₹55–60 Cr

• Target: Breakeven by FY28; PAT profitability by FY29
• Growth aim: 25–30% QoQ despite competition

• Preparing for IPO; engaging institutional investors

• Strategy shift: Profitability over aggressive expansion

• Outlook: Cost discipline + steady growth supports IPO readiness

Smartphones – Cost Inflation Pressure

• 80%+ models see ~15% price hikes
• DRAM/NAND prices up 50%+ YoY
• Entry-level costs (<$200) up ~25%

• Budget segment (<₹15,000) worst hit; prices up 20%+
• Feature downgrades (RAM/storage) visible

• New launches priced 30–40% higher
• Brands passing costs to protect margins

• Demand weak; sales ~9% YoY decline
• Consumers delaying upgrades

• Prices may spike 80–85% in Q2 CY26

• Outlook: Inflation hurting affordability, demand, volumes

Renewable Energy – Transmission Bottleneck

• ~60 GW RE capacity stuck awaiting transmission in Rajasthan
• Applications ~130 GW; only ~73 GW transmission planned
• Grid planning lagging behind rapid RE expansion
• State potential ~179 GW; >85% in Barmer, Bikaner, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur
• Risk to 500 GW non-fossil target by 2030
• Power evacuation delays to demand centres
• CTUIL struggling to scale infra
• Mismatch: generation growth vs grid readiness
• Outlook: Transmission infra key constraint for RE growth

Recycled Metal Exports – Margin Pressure

• War-risk surcharge up to $2,000/container
• Freight to Gulf up to 10x; Europe $3,000–4,000
• Margins (1–2%) wiped out; logistics ~4% of value
• ~₹50 lakh impact for ~25 containers
• CIF contracts limit cost pass-through
• Transit delays: Europe 30–40 → 60–70 days
• Shift to air freight; costs nearly double
• Rising insurance + logistics adding stress
• Outlook: Exports unviable; margin pressure, contract risks

text:
DELHI EV POLICY: MASS MARKET EV ADOPTION ACCELERATOR

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Delhi EV policy strongly favors mass market EV adoption with incentives targeted below ₹30 lakh segment  

STRUCTURAL SHIFT  
- Policy focus shifting from premium EVs to affordable segments  
- Urban EV adoption gaining momentum  
- Strong push via tax waivers and scrappage incentives  
- Higher EV penetration in Delhi vs national average  
- Impact: Positive — demand acceleration in volume segment  

WHY THIS POLICY MATTERS  
- Full road tax and registration waiver reduces upfront cost  
- Scrappage incentive up to ₹1 lakh improves affordability  
- Encourages faster replacement of ICE vehicles  
- Focus on urban mobility electrification  
- Impact: Positive — faster EV adoption curve  

KEY BENEFICIARIES

PASSENGER EV LEADERS  
- Tata Motors Mahindra & Mahindra  
- Strong existing EV portfolio and scale advantage  
- Impact: Major beneficiaries with market share gains  

EARLY EV INVESTORS  
- Companies with established EV ecosystem and supply chain  
- Benefit from first mover advantage  
- Impact: Competitive edge strengthens  

2W EV PLAYERS  
- Bajaj Auto Hero MotoCorp Ather Energy  
- Policy support extends to two wheelers  
- Impact: Volume expansion in urban mobility  

LIMITED BENEFICIARIES

LATE EV ENTRANTS  
- Maruti Suzuki Hyundai  
- Limited EV portfolio presence currently  
- Impact: Near term disadvantage  

HYBRID FOCUSED PLAYERS  
- Companies focusing on hybrid strategy  
- Partial benefit due to policy tilt towards pure EVs  
- Impact: Moderate  

POLICY DESIGN INSIGHT  
- Incentives capped for premium EVs  
- Clear intent to drive mass adoption rather than luxury growth  
- Impact: Volume over value strategy  

MOAT CHARACTERISTICS  
- Early investments in EV technology  
- Established supply chain and ecosystem  
- Scale driven cost advantages  
- Brand recall in EV segment  
- Impact: Strong competitive positioning  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Policy structurally aligns with companies already dominant in affordable EV segment  

OUTLOOK  
- Strong urban EV demand growth  
- Market share consolidation among early leaders  
- Faster electrification of passenger and 2W segment  

IMPACT  
- Positive — Tata Motors & M&M emerge as biggest winners with strong volume tailwinds

Chinese battery makers now control a record 70.4% of global EV battery installed capacity.

China's largest EV battery maker, CATL, alone makes up 39.2% of the global market, surpassing the combined market share of South Korea and Japan.

China is leading the EV battery race more than ever.

Global data center power demand is set to accelerate:

Data center power demand is expected to grow +220% from 2023 levels, or +905 TWh, to a record 1,350 TWh by 2030, according to Goldman Sachs.

This is up from the previously expected +175%, driven by higher AI server shipment projections and increased deployment of more power-intensive servers for AI processing.

~60% of this growth is expected to come from the US, up from ~50% in prior estimates.

As a result, US data center power demand is set to reach ~750 TWh, followed by the rest of the world at ~600 TWh.

Furthermore, US data center capacity is projected to rise +197% between 2025 and 2030, to a record 95 gigawatts.

The AI power boom is accelerating.

Sub: Long Range Forecast for Southwest Monsoon Seasonal Rainfall 2026

(1) India Meteorological Department predicts below normal southwest monsoon seasonal (June to September) rainfall over the country as a whole during 2026.

(2) Quantitatively, the southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole during 2026 is likely to be 92% of long period average (LPA) with a model error of ± 5%. The LPA of the monsoon seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole based on 1971-2020 is 87 cm.

(3) Spatially, below-normal seasonal rainfall is most likely over many parts of country except some areas over Northeast, Northwest and South Peninsular India, where it is likely to be normal to above-normal.

(4) El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO)-neutral conditions are most likely during April to June 2026. Thereafter, El-Nino conditions are very likely during SW Monsoon season.

(5) Neutral Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) conditions are prevailing over the Indian Ocean. Positive IOD conditions are likely to develop towards the end of SW monsoon season which is favourable for SW monsoon seasonal rainfall.

(6) Northern Hemisphere Snow Cover as well as Eurasian Snow Cover during January to March 2026 were slightly below normal, which is favourable for southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall 2026.

Four reasons why:

→ Transformers now have a 5-year lead time (was 2 yrs pre-2020). Prices 5x'd. Only 30-35 facilities globally make high-capacity units.

→ Virginia alone: 60 GW of applications vs 8 GW capacity. Morgan Stanley projects a 49 GW shortfall through 2028.

→ China supplies 40%+ of battery imports and ~30% of switchgear. Tariffs raised the risk, domestic capacity hasn't filled the gap.

→ 25% of 2026 projects haven't even disclosed a power strategy yet.

Result: 16 GW planned for 2026. Only 5 GW actually under construction.

SWARAJ ENGINES: Q4FY26 RESULTS & READ THROUGH FOR M&M

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Strong volume growth and capacity expansion indicate robust tractor demand and forward visibility for M&M farm equipment segment  

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE  
- Revenue: ₹546 Cr  
- PAT: ₹55 Cr (↑ 18% YoY)  
- EBITDA Margin: ~13.7% (stable)  
- Impact: Positive — consistent profitability and margin stability  

VOLUME & DEMAND SIGNAL  
- Q4 Volume: 55,004 engines (highest ever)  
- FY26 Volume: 2,02,771 engines (↑ 19.3% YoY)  
- 6 consecutive years of volume growth  
- Shift towards higher HP tractors improving realizations  
- Impact: Positive — strong rural demand and premiumisation  

READ THROUGH FOR M&M  
- Single customer dependency makes it a direct proxy for Swaraj tractor sales  
- Implies strong performance of M&M farm equipment segment  
- Indicates continued market leadership and demand strength  
- Impact: Positive — leading indicator for M&M earnings  

CAPEX & FUTURE VISIBILITY  
- Capacity expansion to ~2.40 lakh engines annually  
- CWIP increased from ₹4.8 Cr to ₹54.8 Cr  
- Capex up ~185% YoY  
- Impact: Positive — strong demand visibility from M&M  

CASH FLOW CONCERN  
- CFO down ~34% YoY despite PAT growth  
- CFO/PAT ratio at ~0.59x  
- ₹122 Cr increase in receivables  
- Impact: Negative — weaker cash conversion cycle  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Strong demand visibility and capacity expansion suggest M&M preparing for continued tractor growth cycle  

OUTLOOK  
- Sustained growth expected driven by rural demand premiumisation and capacity ramp up  

IMPACT  
- Positive — strong operational performance and forward visibility outweigh short term cash flow concern

text:
INDIA RECYCLING – Structural Shift:

• Rising consumption → higher waste generation → recycling demand surge
• Earlier dominated by informal sector (no tracking, pollution, cash economy)

• Govt push:
EPR norms (2022 onwards) → mandatory recycling responsibility on companies
RCM → removes tax advantage of informal players

• Industry shift: unorganized → organized players

• Key implications:
Better compliance & traceability of waste
Formal players gain scale & pricing power
Improved environmental standards

• Investment theme:
Structural, long-term opportunity
Potential emergence of large listed recycling players

Impact: 🟢 Positive — regulatory push creating multi-year growth opportunity

INDIA DATA CENTRE SECTOR: GROWTH OUTLOOK & OPPORTUNITY

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- India data centre capacity set to expand ~3x by 2030 driven by AI cloud and digital demand  

CAPACITY EXPANSION  
- Current capacity ~1.4–1.6 GW  
- Expected to reach ~5 GW by 2030  
- 700 MW under construction and 1.2 GW in pipeline  
- Additional 2–3 GW expected over next 5–7 years  
- Impact: Positive — strong infra buildout cycle  

MARKET SIZE & INVESTMENT  
- Market size $10 Bn in 2025 → $22 Bn by 2030  
- ~$30 Bn cumulative investment expected by 2026  
- 80% investments from global institutional investors  
- Impact: Positive — strong capital inflow and scalability  

DEMAND DRIVERS  
- AI adoption and high performance computing  
- Hyperscale cloud expansion  
- Digital transformation and 5G rollout  
- Data usage >25 GB per user monthly  
- Impact: Positive — structural demand growth  

COST ADVANTAGE  
- Construction cost $6–7 Mn per MW lower than global peers  
- Competitive power tariffs and labour cost  
- Impact: Positive — global competitiveness  

POLICY SUPPORT  
- Single window clearances and infra status  
- Tax benefits and incentives till 2047  
- State level policies supporting investments  
- Impact: Positive — strong policy backing  

GEOGRAPHIC DISTRIBUTION  
- Mumbai ~49% share dominant hub  
- Chennai 18% NCR 11%  
- Pune 8% Bengaluru 7% Hyderabad 5%  
- Emerging cities including Ahmedabad and others ~1% each  
- Impact: Positive — expansion beyond metros  

TECHNOLOGY SHIFT  
- AI workloads increasing power density and rack intensity  
- Driving new design standards and hyperscale campuses  
- Impact: Positive — higher value infrastructure demand  

KEY INSIGHT  
- India positioned as next global data centre and AI infrastructure hub with strong demand cost advantage and policy support  

OUTLOOK  
- Multi year growth cycle supported by AI cloud and digital economy expansion  

IMPACT  
- Positive — significant opportunity for power utilities data center operators cooling infra and equipment suppliers

MUKUL AGRAWAL PORTFOLIO: Q3 FY26 DECODE

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Portfolio consolidation with higher conviction bets despite small cap slowdown; focus on niche businesses with strong growth visibility  

PORTFOLIO SNAPSHOT  
- Total Value: ₹6,818 Cr (Dec 2025) vs ₹6,753 Cr (Sep 2025)  
- Total Stocks: 64 vs 72 earlier  
- Strategy: Fewer stocks higher concentration  
- Impact: Positive — conviction driven portfolio building  

TOP HOLDINGS & THEMES  
- Neuland Laboratories: API CDMO play with China plus one tailwind  
- ASM Technologies: Semiconductor and engineering exposure high conviction bet  
- Radico Khaitan: Premiumisation in alcohol consumption  
- Zota Healthcare: Affordable healthcare and pharmacy retail  
- Nuvama Wealth Management: Financialisation and wealth management growth  
- Impact: Positive — strong structural themes  

OTHER KEY HOLDINGS  
- PTC Industries: Aerospace and defence manufacturing  
- Arman Financial: Microfinance and credit inclusion  
- Lux Industries Deepak Fertilisers BSE J Kumar Infra InfoBeans Sarda Energy  
- Impact: Diversified exposure across sectors  

Q3 NEW ENTRIES  
- KDDL Ltd: Precision engineering luxury components export driven niche  
- Hindustan Construction Company: Infra capex turnaround play  
- Sundeep Pharma: High margin API and excipient business  
- Allcargo Logistics: Logistics and supply chain play  
- Impact: Positive — early stage entry into emerging opportunities  

STAKE INCREASES  
- Increased exposure in Autoriders NR Agarwal Nuvama Vikran Engineering  
- Vasa Denticity stake increased from 2% to 2.36%  
- Impact: Positive — doubling down on high conviction ideas  

EXITS & DISCIPLINE  
- Exited or reduced stake in 11 companies  
- Stanley Lifestyles stake reduced below 1%  
- Focus on valuation discipline and thesis driven exits  
- Impact: Positive — disciplined portfolio management  

INVESTMENT STRATEGY  
- Focus on underfollowed niche businesses  
- Early entry before market recognition  
- Builds meaningful stakes above 1% reflecting conviction  
- Avoids momentum chasing  
- Impact: Positive — long term wealth creation approach  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Combination of early stage investing high conviction allocation and disciplined exits defines portfolio success  

OUTLOOK  
- Continued focus on structural themes like manufacturing infra financialisation and specialty pharma  

IMPACT  
- Positive — high conviction portfolio with strong multibagger potential driven by niche opportunities

Saudi Arabia is the largest recipient at $82 billion, followed by the UAE at $48 billion, and Iraq at $40 billion.

Furthermore, Chinese investments and construction contracts in Iran have reached $25 billion.

Meanwhile, Middle East trade with China has more than doubled since 2017, to $317 billion in 2024.

That is nearly 4 times the $85 billion in trade with the US in 2024.

China is heavily tied to the Middle East's economy.

DEEPAK NITRITE & DMCC: WHY STOCK IS MOVING UP

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- China export restriction on sulphuric acid creates global supply tightness benefiting domestic chemical players with pricing power  

GLOBAL TRIGGER  
- China to halt sulphuric acid exports from May  
- Impacts metals and fertiliser industries facing supply constraints  
- Leads to tightening global supply and rising prices  
- Impact: Positive — global price tailwind  

PRICING & REALISATIONS  
- Higher global prices support better realizations in acid and downstream derivatives  
- Specialty portfolio enables better pricing vs commodity peers  
- Impact: Positive — margin expansion potential  

CHINA PLUS ONE OPPORTUNITY  
- Supply disruption from China may shift demand to Indian players  
- Potential increase in export opportunities  
- Impact: Positive — demand tailwind  

DOMESTIC OPERATIONS  
- Sulphuric acid plants operating at 95–100% utilization  
- Raw material cost increases passed through  
- Impact: Positive — stable operations with cost control  

SPECIALTY VS COMMODITY MIX  
- Specialty chemicals utilization at ~50% leaving room for scale up  
- Higher margin segment compared to commodity chemicals  
- Europe slowdown impacted specialty utilization  
- Impact: Mixed — short term weakness but long term upside  

EUROPE OUTLOOK  
- Exports declined sharply due to weak demand  
- Management sees this as structural rather than cyclical  
- Impact: Negative — limits near term export growth  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Short term gains driven by global supply disruption while long term upside depends on specialty utilization recovery  

OUTLOOK  
- Near term supported by pricing tailwinds  
- Medium term growth linked to specialty ramp up and export recovery  

IMPACT  
- Positive — supply shock driven margin expansion outweighs regional demand weakness

Cost volatility remains a concern

Impact: Mixed — growth visibility impacted, execution key near term

**INDIA POWER DEMAND – Jefferies View:**

• ⚡️ March’26 demand **↑ 2% YoY**
• 🌧 FY26 demand growth muted at **~1% YoY** (strong monsoon impact)
• 🔮 FY27E demand expected to **recover to ~6% YoY**
• 🌡 **El-Nino (60% probability)** may drive higher power demand

• 📊 Peak demand likely to hit **~270 GW (↑ 10% YoY)** in FY27E
• 🏭 Top picks: **JSW Energy Ltd & NTPC Ltd**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — demand recovery cycle ahead with strong peak outlook

**INDIA INDUSTRIALS – HSBC View:**

• ⚙️ Execution remains **resilient in Q4**, but **margin pressure rising**
• 📉 Headwinds from **input cost inflation & FX volatility**
• 📊 **Order inflow may slow**; Middle East risks to be monitored

**Stock Calls:**

• 🏗 Larsen & Toubro → **Hold | TP ₹3,900**
• ⚡️ ABB India Ltd → **Hold | TP ₹5,400**
• 🔌 Siemens Ltd → **Hold | TP ₹3,230 (cut)**
• 🔋 Cummins India Ltd → **Buy | TP ₹5,500 (↑)**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — strong execution but margin & order risks ahead

**Shriram Finance Ltd – JPMorgan View:**

• 🟢 Recommendation: **Overweight**
• 🎯 Target Price: **₹1,180**

• 🤝 MUFG investment boosts **growth visibility**
• 📉 Structural **cost of funds decline** expected
• 📈 Growth to accelerate via **new segments & better customer retention**
• 📊 **RoA outlook positive**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive

**Solar Industries India Ltd – Goldman Sachs View:**

• 🟢 Recommendation: **BUY**
• 🎯 Target Price: **₹18,900**

• 💣 **Explosives price increase** supports positive outlook
• 🔄 Evidence of **pass-through of higher ammonium nitrate costs**
• 🌍 Likely to pass on costs in **non-defence & international segments**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong pricing power & margin protection

**AUTO SECTOR – Delhi EV Policy 2026 (Morgan Stanley View):**

• ⚡️ Strong **incentives for 2W, 3W & small CV EVs**
• 🚫 Proposed **phase-out timelines for ICE 2W & 3W vehicles**
• 🚗 No direct subsidy for EV cars, but **tax & registration waiver (up to ₹30L)**
• ♻️ Strong hybrids to get **50% tax waiver**

• ⚠️ **Negative for legacy OEMs** if adopted widely across cities
• 🌍 Risk of **policy replication across India**
• 🛑 Implementation challenge: buyers may shift to **NCR for ICE purchases**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — EV ecosystem positive, legacy auto players at risk

Financials: Negative FCF to spike debt and limit PAT growth

**Tata Sons: The Listing Dilemma**

The RBI’s draft guidelines (April 2026) are a game-changer for India's massive shadow banks. By swapping a complex "scoring" model for a flat ₹1 lakh crore asset threshold, the regulator has effectively boxed in large conglomerates. For Tata Sons, with assets near ₹1.75 lakh crore, the "Upper Layer" (NBFC-UL) tag is now definitive, stripping away the ability to lower debt scores to avoid regulation.

The catch? While the classification is clear, the mandatory listing requirement remains a gray area for Core Investment Companies (CICs). After missing the September 2025 deadline, Tata Sons is in a high-stakes standoff: either convince the RBI to accept its registration surrender or bow to mounting pressure from the SP Group and list. If the draft becomes law, the "extraordinary" escape routes are closing fast.

• Integrated “design-to-shelf” apparel player for global premium brands
• 22+ years with Levi’s + clients like Adidas, Tchibo, Bestseller

📊 **Financial Strength**
• Revenue growth: ₹200 Cr → ₹297 Cr (3 years)
• PAT: ₹2.26 Cr → ₹8.45 Cr
• Q3 FY26: Revenue ₹97 Cr | EBITDA ↑ 22% QoQ | PAT ↑ 25% QoQ

⚙️ **Business Model**
• Fully integrated manufacturing → better margins
• 30+ export countries + premium client base
• 46.5 lakh capacity with expansion scope

🌍 **Industry Tailwinds**
• India textile exports target $100B
• Rising demand for premium apparel

🚀 **Growth Triggers**
• “Knit-to-Shape” smart factory (automation + zero waste)
• Capacity expansion + backward integration
• Premium mix improving margins

📈 **Future Roadmap**
• ₹500 Cr revenue target (3 years)
• ₹1,000 Cr in 5 years

🌱 **Sustainability Edge**
• ~50% energy via solar
• Global certifications

💡 **Summary**
• Strong growth + global clients
• High-margin integrated model
• Clear expansion visibility

DATA CENTRE THEME: INDIA BENEFICIARY BASKET

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
• Strong data centre buildout driving demand across power, cooling & precision components  

KEY PLAYERS & THESIS  

Aeroflex Industries  
• Pivot to liquid cooling systems for AI data centres  
• Exclusive 5-year India tie-up with US partner  
• FY29 target: ₹1,000+ Cr revenue | EBITDA: 23–25%  
• Increasing share of high-value products  

TD Power Systems  
• Supplies generators for captive power (20–100 MW range)  
• Beneficiary of shift to captive power by tech firms  
• FY27 revenue: ₹2,200–2,800 Cr potential  

MTAR Technologies  
• Supplies hot boxes for Bloom Energy (fuel cell-based power)  
• Capacity expansion: 8K → 30K units (FY28)  
• Key play on clean, always-on power for data centres  

SECTOR DRIVERS  
• AI-led data centre expansion  
• Need for reliable & captive power  
• Shift toward efficient cooling solutions
